summaries.119532: Arrow-key traversal into MenuAnchor submenus works on non-web platforms but not on web because web shortcuts dispatch ScrollIntent for arrow keys instead of DirectionalFocusIntent. Tab traversal into menus is broken on all platforms. A workaround with custom Actions/DirectionalFocusMenuAction was provided. PR #177159 (draft) was filed by @davidhicks980 and is being finalized.
summaries.143155: The barcode scanner emits a Shift Left key-down event with modifiers: 0 (no modifier flags set), which violates the Flutter assertion that keysPressed must be non-empty before a key-down event. The root cause is a mismatch between what the barcode scanner reports and what Flutter's raw keyboard logic expects. @dkwingsmt confirmed familiarity with similar issues and noted entangled keyboard logic makes investigation difficult; no fix has landed and the issue is stalled.
summaries.107017: Confirmed Android-only (iOS does not reproduce). The bug requires the autofill service to already have stored credentials; commenters struggled to reproduce because they lacked pre-saved autofill data. A Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra tester could not reproduce the autofill hint display at all, suggesting device/autofill-service-specific behavior. No fix or PR mentioned; issue remains open.
summaries.127803: Root cause identified: `isInScribbleRect` is called on TextFields in inactive routes (when both `home` and `initialRoute` are set, two route instances exist and the inactive one's TextField is queried). The fix is to disable Scribble for TextFields that cannot be interacted with. Assigned to @LongCatIsLooong who later indicated it is likely a framework issue; the assignee was eventually removed due to no activity.
summaries.142493: Reproduced on macOS stable and master and also on web (CanvasKit renderer). The issue is that when live conversion is disabled, the delta model receives a replacement with invalid characters (e.g., `^_`) before the correct text is re-inserted. Related issue #149379 was flagged as a possible duplicate. Currently stalled with no fix or PR.
summaries.126263: Comments confirmed the bug is Windows-only (web works correctly; macOS has a related but different issue where text is appended to the end; Linux could not reproduce due to no candidate list). The root cause is that the candidate list is not dismissed when the cursor is moved to a different position during active composition, unlike browser behavior. Confirmed as a regression. A commenter suggested a third-party package `windows_ime_manager` as a workaround. No PR filed.
summaries.122092: Root cause identified: Firefox enables spellcheck on `textarea` elements by default while Chrome disables it, causing inconsistent behavior. Flutter does not set the `spellcheck` HTML attribute on the underlying textarea, so Chrome silently disables it but Firefox enables it. The proper fix requires Flutter to control the `spellcheck` attribute via `SpellCheckConfiguration`; long-term this needs engine-level work. No workaround exists from pure Dart; the JS workaround of setting `spellcheck="false"` directly works but requires a plugin.
summaries.120049: Comments confirmed the cursor positioning and selection issues with RTL text (Persian/Arabic) still reproduced on master after PR #122480 was merged. A community member provided a `CustomTextEditingController` workaround for number-to-Persian-digit conversion but noted the underlying cursor affinity problem persists. No fix landed; assigned to @LongCatIsLooong with no status updates.
summaries.151479: Confirmed iOS-only on mobile web (both CanvasKit and HTML renderers); Chrome on desktop is not affected. The double selection is caused by Safari's native text selection being shown simultaneously with Flutter's selection. A CSS workaround (`-webkit-user-select: none` plus touch event prevention and `BrowserContextMenu.disableContextMenu()`) was shared and found to work for some reporters but not all. Still reproducible in Flutter 3.27.3; no PR filed.
summaries.143479: Confirmed Android-only (iOS does not reproduce). Root cause identified: `_bringIntoViewBySelectionState` in `editable_text.dart` is called on every selection change including handle drags, causing the view to scroll back to the selection end instead of following the drag. The fix is to skip `bringIntoView` when the selection change is caused by a handle drag. Labeled as good first issue. A contributor has confirmed reproduction on current `main` and is investigating a fix with a regression test.
summaries.163606: Root cause identified as a race condition on Android: `showSoftInput()` is called before `InsetsController` fires `hide(ime(), fromIme=false)`, and Android rejects the request until after that event fires. This is more frequent on newer devices and faster host machines. PR #162308 (workaround with retries) was merged; the issue was accidentally closed and reopened. The underlying race condition is still unresolved and tracked alongside flaky test issue #161300.
summaries.117995: Comments confirmed the issue: every character typed calls `requestKeyboard` → `_openInputConnection` → `showSoftInput()` on Android, producing noisy logs. @danagbemava-nc confirmed reproduction. Commenters reported related symptoms (keyboard popping up then hiding, requiring many taps to focus). No root cause analysis or fix mentioned in comments; issue remains open and stalled.
summaries.159171: Root cause explained by @Renzo-Olivares: the engine's `TextInputPlugin` is only aware of the raw text value on the controller, not the `buildTextSpan` override, so key events in the child TextField are processed against the parent's raw text. Additionally, nested TextField focus/keyboard logic has not been wired up to support this pattern. Web works correctly because it handles key events differently. The fix requires engine awareness of `WidgetSpan` lengths. No workaround other than using an Overlay.
summaries.40494: Comments confirmed the bug still reproduces on master (3.6.0+). The root cause is that `FormFieldState.value` is updated after `TextEditingController` listeners fire, so calling `FormState.validate()` inside a controller listener passes the old cached value to validators. A workaround using `scheduleMicrotask` in the listener was proposed. No PR filed; issue remains open.
summaries.79305: Root cause: on web, arrow-key navigation is handled by the browser's engine rather than Flutter's `MoveSelectionUpTextIntent`/`MoveSelectionDownTextIntent`, and the browser's cursor positioning calculation diverges from Flutter's at certain font sizes/renderers. A workaround using `Shortcuts` to override arrow-key intents with Flutter's own `MoveSelectionUp/DownTextIntent` was provided and confirmed working. The issue was reportedly fixed around 3.7 but regressed in 3.10. The team noted it would likely be resolved by #120613 (multiline web text editing improvements).
summaries.72029: Root cause identified: `SelectionOverlay` uses `Overlay`, which renders above all other widgets including AppBars, so there is no z-ordering mechanism to clip handles behind app bars. Confirmed on Android and iOS. One commenter proposed using `ScrollNotificationObserver` in `EditableText` to hide handles when they scroll out of bounds, and has a working prototype. No fix landed; issue is stalled.
summaries.155514: Root cause identified by @justinmc: `TextSelectionOverlay` disposes and recreates its `OverlayEntry`s whenever `contextMenuBuilder` changes (e.g., every rebuild when defined as an inline lambda), and if the `OverlayEntry` is disposed before `_OverlayEntryWidgetState.initState` completes, a null-check crash occurs. Regression introduced by #142463. Workaround: define `contextMenuBuilder` as a static method rather than an inline lambda so it passes equality checks and avoids unnecessary dispose/recreate cycles. Longer-term fix requires refactoring `TextSelectionOverlay` to update values without disposing.
summaries.150338: Root cause identified by a commenter: `_shouldCreateInputConnection` returns `false` for `readOnly: true` on desktop (non-web), which prevents `TextInputConnection` from being created, blocking `onFieldSubmitted` and also breaking Edit menu Copy/Select All on macOS. Web works because `kIsWeb` short-circuits to `true`. A `Shortcuts`-based workaround was provided. The behavior is considered inconsistent with web and may need a design decision on whether `onSubmitted` semantics apply to read-only fields.
summaries.148527: Root cause identified as an intentional engine change in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/49366, which prevents focus loss when the keyboard is collapsed on Android. This broke the pattern where collapsing the keyboard also unfocused the TextField, allowing `requestFocus()` to re-open the keyboard on button press. Users confirmed the regression in 3.22.0 vs 3.19.3. A request was made for a configurable option. No fix or workaround confirmed.
summaries.13182: The root cause is that selection handles are rendered in the root `Overlay`, which paints above all other widgets including AppBars, status bars, and bottom nav bars. Comments documented additional affected surfaces (status bar on iOS, bottom app bar, modal bottom sheet top edge). Proposed solutions include creating an Overlay in the `WidgetsApp` body scope or hiding handles via `ScrollNotificationObserver` when they would overlap UI chrome. A community contributor shared a working prototype using scroll notification detection. No fix landed; stalled.
summaries.80423: Confirmed reproducible on latest stable and master. The flicker/jump is caused by the autofill suggestion window toggling when pressing backspace in an empty field with a number keyboard type combined with autofill hints. The problem also occurs (less severely) with non-numeric keyboards. No root cause analysis beyond the keyboard type + autofill hint interaction. No fix or PR filed; stalled.
